data.title: Bank of Canada rate cut reflects U.S. tariff threat, officials fear permanent GDP hit
data.description:
Bank of Canada officials decided to cut its benchmark interest rate by 25 basis points to 3% last month to better balance inflation risks, and support the economy in light of U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff threat as "the adverse impact on the level of GDP would be permanent."

The policymakers agreed that U.S. tariffs "would weigh on business confidence and investment intentions, as well as consumer sentiment," according to minutes of the monetary policy meeting released on Wednesday.

While there has been some pick up in economic activity, with inflation around 2%, Bank of Canada warned that a protracted trade conflict would lead to weaker GDP and higher prices. The impact would be greater for Canada than the U.S.

"Even if no tariffs were imposed, a long period of uncertainty under the cloud of tariff threats would almost certainly damage business investment in Canada," the minutes stated, as companies are already re-evaluating their investment plans, raising the risk of capital flight from the country to the U.S.

A trade war would slow global growth, which Bank of Canada officials said would reduce energy demand and potentially lower oil prices. "This would tend to lower inflation, but it would also reduce incomes in Canada, given the country's large energy export sector," according to the minutes.

Earlier this month, Trump had announced 25% tariffs on most Canadian goods and 10% duties on energy imports. He later put the tariffs on hold till March 4 after Canada agreed to reinforce border security.

data.title: TEN Holdings announces pricing of $10.0M IPO
data.description:
• TEN Holdings (XHLD) on Thursday announced the pricing of its initial public offering of an aggregate of 1,667,000 shares of common stock for $6.00 per share.
  • The shares are expected to begin trading on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol "XHLD" on February 13.
  • The company expects to receive aggregate gross proceeds of approximately $10.0 million from the offering, before deducting underwriting discounts and other related expenses.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about February 14, 2025.

data.title: UK economy expands 0.1% in Q4 2024, estimates showed
data.description:
The British economy expanded 0.1% in the final quarter of 2024, seeing no growth from the previous quarter, above estimates of a 0.1% contraction, preliminary estimates showed.

On the production side, the services sector increased by 0.2%, construction grew, by 0.5%, while production fell by 0.8%.

On the expenditure side, there were falls in expenditure terms in net trade and gross fixed capital formation that were offset by a large increase in change in inventories.

Monthly GDP in the United Kingdom increased to 0.40% M/M in December from 0.10% in November, a Y/Y surge of 1.5%.

ETFs: (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB)

Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: Bell announces offering of $2.25 billion aggregate principal amount of hybrid notes
data.description:
Bell Canada, a BCE (NYSE:BCE) unit, Thursday announced the offering of $2.25 billion aggregate principal amounts of fixed-to-fixed rate junior subordinated notes in two series.

The $1 billion fixed-to-fixed rate junior subordinated notes, series A due 2055 will initially bear interest at an annual rate of 6.875% and reset every five years starting on September 15, 2030 at an annual rate equal to the five-year U.S. Treasury rate plus a spread of 2.390%.

The $1.25 billion fixed-to-fixed rate junior subordinated notes, series B due 2055 will initially bear interest at an annual rate of 7.000% and reset every five years starting on September 15, 2035 at an annual rate equal to the five-year U.S. Treasury rate plus a spread of 2.363%.

Closing of the offering is expected to occur on February 18, 2025. The notes will be fully and unconditionally guaranteed by BCE (NYSE:BCE).

data.title: Unilever Non-GAAP EPS of €2.98, turnover of €60.8B; initiates FY25 outlook, Ice cream separation on track
data.description:
• Unilever press release (NYSE:UL): FY Non-GAAP EPS of €2.98.
  • Turnover of €60.8B (+2.0% Y/Y). Underlying sales growth of 4.2%, led by 2.9% volume growth.
  • Underlying operating margin up 170bps to 18.4%, with gross margin up 280bps.
  • Cash conversion of 106% with free cash flow of €6.9 billion; underlying ROIC up 190bps to 18.1%.
  • Quarterly dividend raised by 6.1% vs Q4 2023; new €1.5 billion share buyback announced.
  • "The separation of Ice Cream remains on track and we are making good progress on the key workstreams. We announce today the appointment of the Chair Designate for the demerged Ice Cream business and details of the listing structure"
  • Outlook: "We expect underlying sales growth for full year 2025 to be within our multi-year range of 3% to 5%. Market growth slowed throughout 2024. We anticipate a slower start to 2025 with subdued market growth in the near term. We expect the market and our growth to improve during the year as price increases, reflecting higher commodity costs in 2025. We expect a more balanced split between volume and price. We anticipate a modest improvement in underlying operating margin for the full year versus 18.4% in 2024. We expect this improvement to be realised in the second half given the very strong first half comparator of 19.6%, which benefitted strongly from the combination of carry-over pricing and input cost deflation."

data.title: Nestlé S.A. Non-GAAP EPS of CHF 4.77, revenue of CHF 91.35B; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Nestlé S.A. press release (OTCPK:NSRGY): FY Non-GAAP EPS of CHF 4.77.
  • Revenue of CHF 91.35B (-1.8% Y/Y).
  • Organic growth was 2.2%, with a return to positive real internal growth of 0.8%, and both strengthened in the second half.
  • Free cash flow improved to CHF 10.7 billion, and the Board proposes an increase in the dividend per share to CHF 3.05.
  • "From 2025, we expect our actions to drive an improvement in organic sales growth, with a lower underlying trading operating profit margin in the short term as we invest for growth. While there is macroeconomic uncertainty, we have lots of opportunities ahead of us, and we have the strategy, the resources and the people and team to deliver."
  • 2025 outlook unchanged
    • Guidance in line with previous outlook, as accelerated delivery of cost efficiencies offsets recent increases in key commodity prices.
    • Organic sales growth expected to improve compared to 2024, strengthening through the year as we continue to deliver on our growth plans.
    • UTOP margin expected to be at or above 16.0% as we invest for growth.
    • Guidance assumes no significant change in key macroeconomic variables.

data.title: Germany's inflation eases to 2.3% in January
data.description:
• The annual inflation rate in Germany was confirmed at 2.3% in January 2025, easing from 2.6% in December and meeting estimates.

  • Food inflation slowed significantly to 0.8%, compared to 2.0% in the previous month, while prices for services and goods moderated. Meanwhile, energy costs declined by 1.6%, the same pace as in December, mainly due to drops in household energy and motor fuels. Electricity prices also fell.

  • Core inflation, which excludes volatile items such as food and energy, eased to 2.9%, the lowest in three months.

  • On a monthly basis, consumer prices fell by 0.2% in January, reversing a 0.5% increase in December.

  • ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR) (DAX:IND)
  • Currency: (EUR:USD)
